Patch Set 6:

Kyrylo, if this is an existing test failure (i.e. a bug is in Jira already), then please mark the test failure in Maloo with the LU-nnnn ticket number, and add a comment here and we will resubmit the test.

If this is a new test failure, please file a bug with details of the test failure so that it can be tracked and fixed.

You can search in Maloo for similar test failures to see if this subtest has been failing recently:  https://maloo.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/query?utf8=%E2%9C%93&test_set[test_set_script_id]=f9516376-32bc-11e0-aaee-52540025f9ae&sub_test[sub_test_script_id]=12217f84-32be-11e0-b685-52540025f9ae&sub_test[status]=FAIL&sub_test[query_bugs]=&test_session[test_host]=&test_session[test_group]=&test_session[user_id]=&test_session[query_date]=&test_session[query_recent_period]=2419200&test_node[os_type_id]=&test_node[distribution_type_id]=&test_node[architecture_type_id]=&test_node[file_system_type_id]=&test_node[lustre_branch_id]=&test_node_network[network_type_id]=&commit=Update+results

It looks like this only started failing on Jan 15th, possibly because of some patch landing.

Patch Set 6:

Hi,
yeah I can see some failing for sanity_129 similar this one: https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/2418869a-5f87-11e2-b507-52540035b04c

We actually seen this before for long time ago with our test system, and looks, corresponding ticket http://jira.whamcloud.com/browse/LU-2479.

So can we let this patch go already, since failing of the test_129 is no related to it?

Patch Set 7:

Unfortunately, we are not able to land any patches with a failed test result, even if it is for a known failure. We can resubmit patches for retest that have failed a known problem and try to get them to pass. 

This patch is unusual that it is failing sanity.sh test_129 repeatedly, which I wouldn't expect if it is just some random failure. Are you sure there is no correlation between the patch and this failure?  Are you able to pass sanity on your local test machine with this patch? The test_129 has only failed 8 times in the past 4 weeks out of 1000+ test runs, and 4 of them have been with this patch: https://maloo.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/query?utf8=?&test_set%5Btest_set_script_id%5D=f9516376-32bc-11e0-aaee-52540025f9ae&sub_test%5Bsub_test_script_id%5D=12217f84-32be-11e0-b685-52540025f9ae&sub_test%5Bstatus%5D=&sub_test%5Bquery_bugs%5D=&test_session%5Btest_host%5D=&test_session%5Btest_group%5D=&test_session%5Buser_id%5D=&test_session%5Bquery_date%5D=&test_session%5Bquery_recent_period%5D=2419200&test_node%5Bos_type_id%5D=&test_node%5Bdistribution_type_id%5D=&test_node%5Barchitecture_type_id%5D=&test_node%5Bfile_system_type_id%5D=&test_node%5Blustre_branch_id%5D=&test_node_network%5Bnetwork_type_id%5D=&commit=Update+results

Andreas,
Could you please review the http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,5140
If we can land it soon, so the issue with sanity test_129 disappear and this ticket is unblocked.

Patch Set 10: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(39 inline comments)

This patch failed sanity.sh test_129 (LU-2479) again twice, and is again the only patch under test that is failing that test in the past four weeks.  I can't see exactly why that is since this patch does not modify test_129() directly, but it is definitely strange, and cannot just be coincidental.  Hopefully once http://review.whamcloud.com/5140 lands this patch will also pass testing.

Maybe it relates to the change in $tdir causing shorter pathnames for the max_dir_size test?  Just a guess, since I can't see why that should matter.

This patch should be rebased on top of http://review.whamcloud.com/5140 and/or wait for that patch to land.

There are also a number of style issues on the modified lines that should be fixed at the same time.
Uploaded patch set 11.

Patch Set 16: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(59 inline comments)

There are a lot of good cleanups in this patch, nice to see them.  There are probably a lot more test cleanup patches like this that could be made.

Unfortunately, a few of the mkdir->test_mkdir changes will break DNE testing, so they need to be reverted.

A number of further improvements could be made since you are touching these tests anyway.

Note also that this patch is really large, and has a high chance to conflict with other patches that are being landed.  It might make sense to split the core test-framework.sh change along with a few smaller files, and separate the huge change to sanity.sh into its own patch, since that is also the most likely to conflict.  It might also be OK, since it is mostly touching old tests...  It's up to you.

Patch Set 17:

Thanks for the comments. I have a new version of this patch that I will upload shortly and will include your improvements. I will include one or two more tests in the next version of the patch.

Patch Set 17:

James, I would be also happy to land the current patch and have a second patch to do further cleanups. Otherwise, the patch gets large and likely to conflict with _something_ and be difficult to land.

Since this patch has failed testing anyway, no harm to improve it a bit more, but I'm always reluctant to discard good test results for a cleanup patch like this and get into a treadmill of holding the patch back for minor improvements.

Patch Set 19: Code-Review+1

(6 comments)

To check code style errors, please perform the following commands from the top-level Lustre tree checkout:

 ln -sf ../../build/commit-msg .git/hooks/
 ln -sf ../../build/prepare-commit-msg .git/hooks/

Then at "git commit" time this will run the build/prepare-commit-msg script from the currently Lustre tree to check the code changes for style errors using build/checkpatch.pl, and add comments at the end of the commit message with any warnings or errors.

parents.subject: LU-4704 acl: fix permission problem of setfacl

commit.subject: LU-2524 test: Modify tdir variable and code clean up (1)
commit.message:
LU-2524 test: Modify tdir variable and code clean up (1)

The patch modifying the tdir variable to a single directory
has landed; http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/8123/. We can
now remove the `-p` option from many instances of mkdir and
conduct other miscellaneous cleanup including:

Removing the `-p` (parents) option from many calls to mkdir
Replace `lfs setstripe` with $SETSTRIPE
Replace `lfs getstripe` with $GETSTRIPE
Added check for and call `error` and/or added error messages for
a variety of common functions.
Replace `…` with $(...)
Removed linefeed escape after |, ||, & and && operators.
Modified directory and file names to use $tdir and $tfile

Signed-off-by: Kyrylo Shatskyy <kyrylo_shatskyy@xyratex.com>
Signed-off-by: James Nunez <james.a.nunez@intel.com>
Change-Id: Icece27ec7dd04117d3ab586e8da1434dc6ed0e51
